subject: Add VFC containers to ONAP K8S
commit_message:
Add VFC containers to ONAP K8S

Add VFC containers to ONAP K8S

MK:- fixed issues related to port names longer than 15chars
   - scoped pods to the onap-vfc namespace.
All deployments still fail due to missing docker images in nexus3

Yan:- fixed docker images in nexus3

MK (patchset 7)
 - adding image pull secrets or they all fail
 - address Alexis comments by removing trailing spaces &
   removing activii (with the comments left in it crashes out)
MK: (patchset 8)
 - removing trailing whitespaces

Change-Id: Iab9f641aaa580511c0e4a073a71da723aaf07315
Issue-ID: OOM-19
Signed-off-by: maopengzhang <zhang.maopeng1@zte.com.cn>
Signed-off-by: Mandeep Khinda <mandeep.khinda@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Do we need to add MSB service end point annotations in all-services.yaml ?
Patch Set 4: Published edit on patch set 3.

Patch Set 4: Verified-1

When trying to bring this up it fails:
Error: release onap-vfc failed: Deployment.apps "vfc-hwvnfmdriver" is invalid: spec.template.spec.containers[0].ports[0].name: Invalid value: "vfc-hwvnfmdriver": must be no more than 15 characters

Patch Set 4:

pods will not come up without valid docker images in nexus3.onap.org...

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1 Verified+1

As of PS #7, the helm can start the pods however they do not fully start.
At this point, this is a docker image level problem and I think we can merge this in and resolve the rest through bugs.  What does everyone think?
